answer: PostgreSQL can handle 10TB datasets efficiently with proper hardware and tuning; costs depend on instance type and region. Scaling beyond 10TB requires advanced techniques and optimizations. Monthly costs vary significantly based on infrastructure and usage.

results.title: How We Scaled PostgreSQL to 350 TB+ (With 10B New Records/Day)
results.snippet: 01 Scaling PostgreSQL to Analyze Queries Over our Entire Fleet02 How We Built It: Powering Real-Time Analytics With a (Huge) Database03 Powering (fast) real-time analytics04 Storing hundreds of TBs in a single Timescale service05 Scaling a 350 TB+ PostgreSQL Database Using Timescale: Lessons Learned06 Wrap-Up. 01 Scaling PostgreSQL to Analyze Queries Over our Entire Fleet02 How We Built It: Powering Real-Time Analytics With a (Huge) Database03 Powering (fast) real-time analytics04 Storing hundreds of TBs in a single Timescale service05 Scaling a 350 TB+ PostgreSQL Database Using Timescale: Lessons Learned06 Wrap-Up. Earlier this year, we embarked on our company’s largest dogfooding effort ever. To build Insights, a tool that allows our users to analyze their query performance within the Timescale platform, we sought to collect query statistics across all our customers’ databases, including timing, CPU, memory usage, and disk I/O related to each and every query.

results.title: Postgres RDS is too expensive - : r/devops - Reddit
results.snippet: We run a multi az rds postgres on a db.m6g.12xl instance with 10tb of storage in ap-south-1. This is costing us 12k USD per month.
